subject: Human Resources
title: Sales Commissions
description: A short lesson to help you apply Sales Commissions.

hook.text: A pharmaceutical sales rep earns $300K in commissions—more than the CEO of a small company. Commissions can be enormous.

buildup.text: Commissions pay salespeople a percentage of revenue they generate—directly linking income to results.

discovery.text: Commission structures vary: straight commission, base plus commission, tiered rates, and accelerators above targets.

twist.text: Poorly designed commissions drive bad behavior—reps oversell, churn customers, or game quotas at quarter's end.

climax.text: The best plans balance short-term sales incentives with long-term customer retention and ethical selling metrics.

punchline.text: Commissions drive behavior. Design them for the behavior you want.

quiz.question: What risk do poorly designed commission structures create?
options: They incentivize bad behavior like overselling and gaming quotas
